---
status: draft
shortDescription: "Lifetime of variables declared in a loop body extends across all loop iterations"
shortCorrection: "Lifetime of variables declared in a loop body is limited to one loop iteration"
concepts:
  - Loop
  - Variable
  - Lifetime
  - Scope
related:
- id: ForVariableScopeBeyondLoop
  relation: Not about lifetime, but about scope
# - id: LiteralNoExpression
#   relation: Special case
inOtherPl:
# - pl: JavaScript
#   id: LoopBodyScopeImpliesLoopLifetime
# - pl: Python
#   id: LoopBodyScopeImpliesLoopLifetime
spec:
- id: jls13
  sections: ["4.12.3", "14.4", "6.3"]
plVersion:
# from: jls8
# to: jls14
---
Declaring a variable in a loop body means
that its lifetime extends throughout all iterations of the loop.
If we store a value in that variable in one iteration,
this value will still be there in the next iteration.

frontmatter.shortDescription: Lifetime of variables declared in a loop body extends across all loop iterations
frontmatter.shortCorrection: Lifetime of variables declared in a loop body is limited to one loop iteration
